entry.title: How do I consume/display the data of Odata on Fiori App?
entry.content:
How do I consume/display the OData on my Fiori App? I've run my fiori app in local

Result in Gateway Client in SAP GUI:

 

Result in Fiori App:

I was expected sales document number instead of Chang_121 and user instead of Changed_121.

entry.title: How to send a high priority mail as in outlook from ABAP
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to send  a high priority mail from ABAP OData. I am currently using cl_bcs class. I have tried setting the priority in SODOCCHGI1 as 1. But still i am not able to receive mail with high importance flag.

entry.title: Creating Sales Orders with Billing Plans and Delivery Dates via API
entry.content:
While working on an integration to create sales orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, I came across two relevant OData APIs provided by SAP:

  • OData V4: CE_SALESORDER_0001

  • OData V2: API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

I encountered some unexpected limitations and I am hoping you could help me with a best practice approach. 

The Problem

Goal:

  • Create a Sales Order via API

  • Include a billing plan on item level

  • Include the requested delivery date on item level

What I Found

V2 – API_SALES_ORDER_SRV

  • Supports billing plan at item level 

  • Does not support Requested Delivery Date at item level directly in the item payload 

    • Strangely enough, the “Create Sales Order” Fiori app does let you enter requested delivery dates per item manually

    • You can only set it via a second call to the schedule line entity (to_ItemScheduleLine)

V4 – CE_SALESORDER_0001

  • No support for billing plans 

  • Does support Requested Delivery Date per item

The Workaround I’m Using

Since I want both the billing plan and requested delivery date, I’m forced to:

  1. Use V2 API to create the Sales Order with Billing Plan.

  2. Make a second API call to /A_SalesOrderItemScheduleLine to update the RequestedDeliveryDate.

My Questions

  • Why is OData V4, a newer version, missing such key functionality like billing plans?
  • Is there a reason why requested delivery date is not available directly in the item payload in V2?
  • Are there other options or best practices to handle this more efficiently?
  • Is there a roadmap for V2? Or will changes only apply for newer versions?

entry.title: Issue in Creating SAP Fiori Elements List Report Using Parameterized CDS View using OData V4 UI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently developing a Fiori Elements List Report based on a parameterized CDS view in RAP. We have created a parameterized consumption CDS view and binding it using an OData V4 UI service. 

While generating the Fiori Elements List Report application, only the parameters are visible in the App as output fields, and no filters are available. While executing it, we are getting the below error.

 

Below is the CDS View which is created. The parameters are exposed from the base level CDS

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption View for Proj Fin Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION
define view entity ZPTD_C_PROJ_FIN_REP
  with parameters
    p_bukrs      : bukrs,
    p_perio_from : co_perio,
    p_perio_to   : co_perio,
    p_gjahr_from : gjahr,
    p_gjahr_to   : gjahr,
    p_pc1        : versn,
    p_pc2        : versn
  as select from ZPTD_I_PROJ_FIN_REP( p_bukrs:$parameters.p_bukrs,
                                      p_perio_from:$parameters.p_perio_from ,
                                      p_perio_to:$parameters.p_perio_to ,
                                      p_gjahr_from:$parameters.p_gjahr_from ,
                                      p_gjahr_to:$parameters.p_gjahr_to,
                                      p_pc1:$parameters.p_pc1 ,
                                      p_pc2:$parameters.p_pc2  )
{

While creating an OData V2 service, it is working, and the report is showing all the filters and data is displayed.

Is there any workaround to achieve this using OData V4? We are on S4HANA OP2023 version.

Thanks,

Faiz

entry.title: SAP Std API Odata V2 Consumption via Service Consumption Model
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I am in an "SAP S/4 Private cloud (10/2024) without BTP". Focus is clean core. For a given Standard Odata V2 API ( eg Business Partner A2X) how to consume this API?

1: Typical way through SEGW & Service implementation is discouraged as it does not support cleancore . To keep it simple , I dont want to implement anything in Tier3. 

2: I cannot consume it via Service Consumption model as this supports only V4. Moreover, I dont even the see the option of Odata during Service consumption model generation.  Creating Service Consumption Model | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: CI odata adapter: Edm.Guid 'Wrong literal format for literal'
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I'm building an iFlow using oData adapters (v2) in integration suite. When trying to update (PUT) the record adapter returns error:

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: Wrong literal format for literal: 'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]'

I tried to workaround this problem by:

  • using batch (same error);
  • changing local edmx file and GUID type field from Edm.Guid to Edm.String and using guid'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]' (remote validation fails with Cannot convert guid'f5cc9649-...' to java.util.UUID
  • Playing with Resource Path in adapter's config - changing it to ${property.path} and setting hardcoded value: entityName(GuID=guid'f292992-aaaa-...') but same metadata validation

GUID is defined as:

<Key>
<PropertyRef Name="GuID"/>
</Key>
<Property Name="GuID" Type="Edm.Guid" Nullable="false" sap:display-format="UpperCase" sap:label="UUID" sap:quickinfo="16-byte UID in 32 chars (hexadecimal)" sap:updatable="false"/>

And using it manually (postman) is possible and works. I found that CI produces incorrect requests (in batch), skiping 'guid' part in key definition. Example batch requests, looks like this:

PUT entityName(GuID='f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]') HTTP/1.1

 instead of (which would work):

PUT entityName(GuID=guid'f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]') HTTP/1.1

So either key validation fails in adapter or (with batch) in backend. How to make it work? I tried sap.support and adapter's configuration but there's nothing about such problems. 

Here's my payload I'm testing with:

<entitySetName>
<entityName>
<GuID>f5cc9649-59ab-1[...]</GuID>
<ProcessedAt>1990-01-26T06:40:03.02</ProcessedAt>
</entityName>
</entitySetName>

And adapter's config:

entry.title: Tree Table loads data only in client operationModeand not in server operationMode
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

I am developing a tree table with basic odata binding and tree annotations(same as given in SDK). However in my case  data is shown only when I use operationMode:'Client'.

 need to filter data based on some Phases, but with  operationMode : client, the filter is ignored.

And when I use operationMode:Server, data is fetched from the backend ,but the table appers blank.

Please find below the xml code -

 <TreeTable id="TreeTableBasic" rowActionCount="1" rows="{

            path : '/MasterscreenSet',      

            parameters : {

            countMode: 'Inline',

            operationMode:'Client',

            numberOfExpandedLevels: '{helperJson>/numberOfExpandedLevels}',

                treeAnnotationProperties : {

                    hierarchyLevelFor : 'HierarchyLevel',

                    hierarchyNodeFor : 'NodeID',

                    hierarchyParentNodeFor : 'ParentNodeID',

                    hierarchyDrillStateFor : 'DrillState'

                }

            }

        }"

        enableSelectAll="false" selectionMode="None" ariaLabelledBy="title" class="teamTableText" rowMode="Interactive" enableBusyIndicator="true"

        busyIndicatorDelay="0" dataReceived="onDataReceived">

Thanks & Regards,

Aastha

SAPUI5 OData  SAP Fiori Makers

entry.title: "Method not Allowed" when posting Fixed Asset API
entry.content:
I'm trying to create a Fixed Asset using OData v4 https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_FIXEDASSET_0001-v1/resource/Fixed_Asset in Public Cloud.

I've created a service consumption model and try to use POST method. However, I'm getting a "Method Not Allowed" error.

I think error is due to the uri path should contain "/SAP__self.CreateMasterFixedAsset" but I cant find a way to squeeze in this path. Coz when I tested using postman with this uri path, it works.

How can I add the mentioned uri path when calling service consumption model?

ls_business_data = VALUE #(

company_code = <lfs_asset>-CompanyCode

* master_fixed_asset = 'MasterFixedAsset'

* fixed_asset = 'FixedAsset'

asset_is_for_post_capitali = abap_false

asset_class = <lfs_asset>-AssetClass

fixed_asset_description = 'FixedAssetDescription'

fixed_asset_assgmt = VALUE #( ( company_code = <lfs_asset>-CompanyCode

validity_end_date = '99991231'

validity_start_date = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( )

cost_center = <lfs_asset>-CostCenter

profit_center = <lfs_asset>-ProfitCenter

segment = <lfs_asset>-Segment ) ) ).

 

" Navigate to the resource and create a request for the create operation

lo_request = lo_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set( 'FIXED_ASSET' )->create_request_for_create( ).

 

DATA(lo_data_description) = lo_request->create_data_descripton_node( ).

lo_data_description->set_properties( lt_prop_asset ).

 

DATA(lo_item_child) = lo_data_description->add_child( 'FIXED_ASSET_ASSGMT' ).

lo_item_child->set_properties( lt_deep_prop_assign ).

 

lo_request->set_deep_business_data(

io_data_description = lo_data_description

is_business_data = ls_business_data

).

 

" Execute the request

lo_response = lo_request->execute( ).

 

" Get the after image

lo_response->get_business_data(

IMPORTING

es_business_data = ls_business_data

).

entry.title: How can I update an OData-Service (/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE) after changes made to my custom CDS view?
entry.content:
Subject: How to Reflect CDS View Changes in an OData Service

Dear Community,

I am currently exploring the integration of a CDS view with an OData service and a UI5 web application.

Current Setup:

  • I created a CDS view based on the SAP table SFLIGHT.
  • I registered the corresponding OData service using transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.
  • Using SAP Business Application Studio, I generated a basic UI5 List Report Page application.

Question:

In the /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE transaction, I see two options:

  1. Add Service
  2. Delete Service

However, I do not see an option to update or refresh the service after making changes to the CDS view.

How can I ensure that modifications to the CDS view are reflected in the OData service?
Is there a specific step required to regenerate or reactivate the service?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
UI5 Beginner

entry.title: Can't get the value from OData v4 entity set
entry.content:
I created a new user and retrieved data from entity set 'BANK'.  But the return value is empty even the entity set has value.

 

It can retrieved data from entity set 'ZCDS_ZTALCUSTOMER'

Another sap-all user can retrieve data from entity set 'BANK'.. 

 

So I doubt there is any authorization  is forgotten. Do you have any idea?

entry.title: Fiori app - F2229 - Adding Custom Fields to Standard OData and execute MERGE call method to update
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement of extending Standard OData with custom fields in the Fiori App F2229, we have completed displaying the fields on a new object page sub section on the Object Page.

But for UPDATE scenario, we're facing an issue on the MERGE call method to update the custom fields on a button click event, in the Gateway system we've found it's working fine using a PUT call method, but to update a specific custom field item even the standard behaviour suggests us a MERGE call method only.

We tried to copy the standard MERGE call method and added the custom field data, it results in success -> 204 no content message but not reflecting in the draft table. Any suggestion on the above issue.

Regards,

Jai

entry.title: bydesign - how to get bill to address for customer in odata
entry.content:
Hi,

I have been unable to grab Customers & Bill To Addresses using oData.

Has anybody managed to get this please ?

entry.title: OData V2 Adapter is not working in Latest CDS Version 9
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I had created a sample CAP JAVA project by referring this link https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-cap-java-app-studio.html it worked fine in OData adapter V4. Then I changed the OData adapter version from v4 to v2 by referring this link https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/migration#v2adapter While I am trying access the metadata I am facing "Service Unavailable" Exception. Is OData V2 is not supported for latest CDS Versions?

entry.title: Facing F4 Help issue in object page of RAP Application
entry.content:
SAP System version - S4 HANA 2022
system's - SAP UI5 version - 1.108
code snippet -

Please help me to solve this issue.
I have used Odata v2 where im facing this issue, but in Odata V4 there is no issue.

entry.title: How to upload Classification Characteristic Values to Document Info Record
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

the API Documents | Document Info Record | SAP Business Accelerator Hub  allows the creation of document info records using an OData Service. 

The API also allows the retrieval of the classification of a document info record (" Item node that contains classification values. Only Read operation is supported ")

We currently have an option to create the document info record and upload/link attachments via an API via an connected system. But the maintenance of the of classification of the document and valuation of the characteristics must be done manual in the system. 

Is there also an API to maintain values to a classification characteristic? 

Thank you for the help.

Florian

entry.title: Unsupported media type 415 error when using cap-js/attachment via HTTP POST
entry.content:
I'm trying to upload an attachment via HTTP POST OData call to my CAP service entity that has an extended Attachments field/association from cap-js/attachments via a React external app, but I'm encountering a 415 HTTP error code. I can successfully create an entry to my main entity, but I'm unable to create an attachment due to the 415 error. Has anyone encountered this?

So far the terminal logs isn't showing any error, only in the browser.

entry.title: Reading GOS(Generic Object Services) in MM03
entry.content:
Hi  @everyone , Recently I was working on an small project where i have to develop an application where you can create, read and delete GOS attachment in mm03 for specific material.

This post is specific to reading GOS in mm03 using material and business object type.

   DATA : it_tab   TYPE /iwbep/s_mgw_name_value_pair,          
          gv_matnr TYPE matnr.
    DATA: gs_lpor TYPE sibflporb.
    DATA: lt_relat   TYPE obl_t_relt,
          la_relat   LIKE LINE OF lt_relat,
          lt_xstring TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF xstring,
          lv_xstring TYPE xstring.
    DATA: t_links  TYPE obl_t_link,
          la_links LIKE LINE OF t_links.
    DATA: lo_root TYPE REF TO cx_root,
          l_folder_id TYPE soodk,
          l_object_id TYPE soodk,
          document_id TYPE sofmk.
    DATA : range_ls_matnr TYPE RANGE OF matnr,
           ls_matnr       LIKE LINE OF range_ls_matnr,
           doc_id         TYPE sibfboriid.
    DATA : it_sort_data TYPE SORTED TABLE OF zzstructure
            WITH UNIQUE KEY matnr image,
           it_data      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zzstructure.
           wa_data      TYPE zzstructure.
    DATA: et_attachments     TYPE zzstructure(line types),
          et_attach_bid_quot TYPE zzstructure,
          et_attach          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zzstructure(strucure).

    DATA : ls_select_option         TYPE /iwbep/s_mgw_select_option,
           ls_select_internal_table TYPE /iwbep/t_cod_select_options,
           wa_select_option         TYPE /iwbep/s_cod_select_option.

 " we will have matnr as input here i have an odata service which is 
 " get entity set which is providing all the gos attachemnt in that material 
 " in mm03. 
READ TABLE it_filter_select_options INTO ls_select_option WITH KEY property = 'Matnr'.
    IF ls_select_option-property IS NOT INITIAL.
      ls_select_internal_table[] = ls_select_option-select_options[].

      LOOP AT ls_select_internal_table INTO wa_select_option.
        ls_matnr-sign = wa_select_option-sign.
        ls_matnr-option = wa_select_option-option.
        ls_matnr-low = wa_select_option-low.
        ls_matnr-high = wa_select_option-high.
        gv_matnr = ls_matnr-low.
        APPEND ls_matnr TO range_ls_matnr.
        CLEAR : wa_select_option , ls_matnr.
      ENDLOOP.
*      gv_matnr = it_tab-value.
      gs_lpor-instid = gv_matnr.
      gs_lpor-typeid = 'BUS1001006'.
      gs_lpor-catid  = 'BO'.
      la_relat-sign = 'I'.
      la_relat-option = 'EQ'.
      la_relat-low = 'ATTA'.
      APPEND la_relat TO lt_relat.

      doc_id = CONV sibfboriid( gv_matnr ).

      CALL FUNCTION 'ZREADGOS'
        EXPORTING
          business_object    = 'BUS1001006'
          document_number    = doc_id
        IMPORTING
          et_attachments     = ET_aTTACHMENTS
          et_attach_bid_quot = et_attach_bid_quot.

      DATA: ls_attachment TYPE ZSREAD_GOS,
            output        TYPE string.

*READ TABLE et_attachments INTO ls_attachment INDEX 1.
*IF sy-subrc = 0.
*  lv_xstring = ls_attachment-file_content. " Assuming file_content is of type XSTRING
*ENDIF.
      LOOP AT et_attachments INTO ls_attachment.
        IF ls_attachment-file_content IS NOT INITIAL.
          lv_xstring = ls_attachment-file_content.
          CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BASE64_ENCODE_STR'
            EXPORTING
              input  = lv_xstring
            IMPORTING
              output = output.
          wa_data-brelguid = ls_attachment-brelguid.
          wa_data-instid_a = ls_attachment-instid_a.
          wa_data-instid_b = ls_attachment-instid_b.
          wa_data-roletype_a = ls_attachment-roletype_a.
          wa_data-typeid_a   = ls_attachment-typeid_a.
          wa_data-typeid_b = ls_attachment-typeid_b.
          wa_data-name =    ls_attachment-name.
          wa_data-image = lv_xstring.
          wa_data-base  = output.
          wa_data-matnr = gv_matnr.
          APPEND wa_data TO it_data.
        ENDIF.
        CLEAR : lv_xstring , wa_data , output , ls_attachment.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

 

Reading the gos fm :

FUNCTION ZREADGOS.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(BUSINESS_OBJECT) TYPE  SIBFTYPEID
*"     VALUE(DOCUMENT_NUMBER) TYPE  SIBFBORIID
*"  EXPORTING
*"     VALUE(ET_ATTACHMENTS) TYPE  ZSTRUCURE
*"     VALUE(ET_ATTACH_BID_QUOT) TYPE  ZSTRUCTURE
*" ZSTRUCTURE : 
*"  BRELGUID	Types	OS_GUID	RAW
*"  ROLETYPE_A	Types	OBLROLTYPE	CHAR
*"  INSTID_A	Types	SIBFBORIID	CHAR
*"  TYPEID_A	Types	SIBFTYPEID	CHAR
*"  INSTID_B	Types	SIBFBORIID	CHAR
*" TYPEID_B	Types	SIBFTYPEID	CHAR
*" MATNR	Types	MATNR	        CHAR
*" FILE_NAME	Types		        STRING
*" FILE_TYPE	Types		        STRING
*" FILE_CONTENT	Types		        RAWSTRING
*" FILE_EXT	Types	SAEDOKTYP	CHAR
*" ERDAT	Types	ERDAT	        DATS
*" ERNAM	Types	ERNAM	        CHAR
*" NAME	        Types		        STRING
*" MESSAGE	Types		        STRING
MESSAGE_TYPE	Types		STRING
*"
*"

*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
  DATA : ls_lporb       TYPE sibflporb,
         lt_link        TYPE obl_t_link,
         ls_link        TYPE LINE OF obl_t_link,
         lv_document_id TYPE  sofolenti1-doc_id.
  DATA : mime      TYPE mimetypes-type,
         lt_string TYPE string_table,
         ls_string TYPE string,
         lv_line   TYPE i.
  DATA : lo_header    TYPE REF TO cl_bcs_objhead,
         lv_filename1 TYPE string.

  DATA lt_object_content  TYPE swftlisti1.
  DATA lt_contents_hex    TYPE solix_tab.
  DATA lv_xstring         TYPE xstring.
  DATA lv_filename        TYPE string.
  DATA lv_mime_type       TYPE string.
  DATA: lo_wi_api         TYPE REF TO if_swr_workitem_api.
  DATA: lt_rfc           TYPE swrtsystem,
        ls_rfc           TYPE swrssystem,
        ls_document_data TYPE sofolenti1.
  DATA : lt_object_header TYPE TABLE OF solisti1.
  DATA : ls_object_header TYPE solisti1.
  DATA: ls_attachment TYPE ZSREAD_GOS.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_contents_hex>   LIKE LINE OF lt_contents_hex.

  ls_lporb-instid = document_number.
  ls_lporb-typeid = business_object.
  ls_lporb-catid = 'BO'.

  TRY.
      CALL METHOD cl_binary_relation=>read_links_of_binrel
        EXPORTING
          is_object   = ls_lporb
          ip_relation = 'ATTA'
          ip_role     = 'GOSAPPLOBJ'
        IMPORTING
          et_links    = lt_link.
    CATCH cx_root.
  ENDTRY.


  LOOP AT lt_link INTO ls_link.
    lv_document_id = ls_link-instid_b.
    CALL FUNCTION 'SO_DOCUMENT_READ_API1'
      EXPORTING
        document_id                = lv_document_id
      IMPORTING
        document_data              = ls_document_data
      TABLES
        object_header              = lt_object_header
        object_content             = lt_object_content
        contents_hex               = lt_contents_hex
      EXCEPTIONS
        document_id_not_exist      = 1
        operation_no_authorization = 2
        x_error                    = 3
        OTHERS                     = 4.

* build xstring for binary file
    IF lt_contents_hex[] IS NOT INITIAL.
      LOOP AT lt_contents_hex ASSIGNING <fs_contents_hex>.
        CONCATENATE lv_xstring <fs_contents_hex>-line INTO lv_xstring IN BYTE MODE.
      ENDLOOP.
      IF lt_object_header[] IS NOT INITIAL.
        lo_header = cl_bcs_objhead=>create( lt_object_header ).
        IF  lo_header  IS BOUND.
          lv_filename1 = lo_header->get_filename( ).
          CALL FUNCTION 'ZZFILENAME'
            EXPORTING
              complete_filename = lv_filename1
            IMPORTING
              extension         = ls_attachment-file_ext
              name              = ls_attachment-file_name
            EXCEPTIONS
              invalid_drive     = 1
              invalid_path      = 2
              OTHERS            = 3.
          CLEAR lv_filename1.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ELSEIF lt_object_content[] IS NOT INITIAL.
              lo_header = cl_bcs_objhead=>create( lt_object_header ).
        IF  lo_header  IS BOUND.
          lv_filename1 = lo_header->get_filename( ).
        endif.
        CALL FUNCTION 'ZZFILENAME'
            EXPORTING
              complete_filename = lv_filename1
            IMPORTING
              extension         = ls_attachment-file_ext
              name              = ls_attachment-file_name
            EXCEPTIONS
              invalid_drive     = 1
              invalid_path      = 2
              OTHERS            = 3.

          CLEAR lv_filename1.
      CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_TEXT_TO_XSTRING'
        IMPORTING
          buffer   = lv_xstring
        TABLES
          text_tab = lt_object_content
        EXCEPTIONS
          failed   = 1
          OTHERS   = 2.
    ENDIF.

    ls_attachment-file_content = lv_xstring.
    ls_attachment-erdat = ls_document_data-creat_date.
    ls_attachment-name = ls_document_data-creat_fnam.
    ls_attachment-brelguid = ls_link-brelguid.
    ls_attachment-instid_a = ls_link-instid_a.
    ls_attachment-instid_b = ls_link-instid_b.
    ls_attachment-matnr = CONV matnr( document_number ).
    ls_attachment-roletype_a = ls_link-roletype_a.
    ls_attachment-typeid_a = ls_link-typeid_a.
    ls_attachment-typeid_b = ls_link-typeid_b.

TRANSLATE ls_attachment-file_ext TO UPPER CASE."pHANIDRA
    SELECT SINGLE mimetype FROM toadd
      INTO ls_attachment-file_type
       WHERE doc_type EQ ls_attachment-file_ext.

    APPEND ls_attachment TO et_attachments.

    CLEAR:lt_contents_hex[], lv_document_id, ls_document_data,
          lt_object_content[], ls_link, lv_xstring, ls_attachment .
  ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDFUNCTION.

 FM ZZFILENAME :

FUNCTION ZZFILENAME.
*"--------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(COMPLETE_FILENAME) TYPE  STRING
*"     VALUE(CHECK_DOS_FORMAT) LIKE  RC27X-FLG_SEL OPTIONAL
*"  EXPORTING
*"     VALUE(DRIVE) LIKE  PCFILE-DRIVE
*"     VALUE(EXTENSION)
*"     VALUE(NAME)
*"     VALUE(NAME_WITH_EXT)
*"     VALUE(PATH) TYPE  STRING
*"  EXCEPTIONS
*"      INVALID_DRIVE
*"      INVALID_PATH
*"--------------------------------------------------------------------

*................ Local Data Declaration  ........................*

  DATA: offset    TYPE i,              "// length of the string

        head   TYPE string,       "// Drive
        tail   TYPE string,       "// Path
        puffer TYPE string OCCURS 0 WITH HEADER LINE,
                                    "// Internal Table to hold the paths
        dot(1) TYPE c.                 "// Dot Extension

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <c>.

*......................................................................*


*-- Convert the filename from '/' to '\',if required                 --*

  TRANSLATE complete_filename USING '/\'.
  tail = complete_filename.

  IF tail CS ':'.
*-- Split the filename into drive and filepath-------------------------*

    SPLIT tail AT ':' INTO head tail.

*-- Check whether drive is valid or not                              --*

    offset = strlen( head ).
    IF offset > 1.
      RAISE invalid_drive.
    ENDIF.

    drive = head.

    CALL FUNCTION 'PC_CHECK_DRIVE'
         EXPORTING
              idrive        = drive
         IMPORTING
              edrive        = drive
         EXCEPTIONS
              invalid_drive = 1.

    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RAISE invalid_drive.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

*-- Get the filename and path------------------------------------------*
  SPLIT tail AT '\' INTO TABLE puffer.
  offset = strlen( tail ) - 1.
  SHIFT tail BY offset PLACES LEFT.
  IF tail = '\'.
    APPEND INITIAL LINE TO puffer.
  ENDIF.
  CLEAR tail.
  DESCRIBE TABLE puffer LINES sy-tfill.
  offset = sy-tfill - 1.
  LOOP AT puffer TO offset.
    CONCATENATE tail puffer '\' INTO tail.
  ENDLOOP.
  DESCRIBE TABLE puffer LINES sy-tfill.
  READ TABLE puffer INDEX sy-tfill.

  IF puffer IS INITIAL.
*-- Check whether the filename is present or not-------*
    CLEAR name.
    CLEAR name_with_ext.
  ELSE.
    CLEAR dot.
*-- Check whether the filename has an extension or not     --*

    IF NOT puffer CS '.'.
*-- Transfer only the name value (no extension present)----------------*
      name = puffer.
      CLEAR extension.
*-- Transfer the name and extension value  ---------------------*
    ELSE.
      dot = '.'.
      SPLIT puffer AT '.' INTO name extension.
    ENDIF.
*-- Build the filename with extension  ---------------------*

    CONCATENATE name dot extension INTO name_with_ext.
  ENDIF.
*-- Check the path of the file ?  ---------------------*

  offset = strlen( tail ).
  IF offset > 0.

*-- Convert the filename from '/' to '\',if required  ---*
        TRANSLATE tail USING '/\'.
        path = tail.

  ENDIF.



ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Deleting GOS in MM03
entry.content:
This is followed post of reading GOS:

Reading GOS(Generic Object Services) in MM03 - SAP Community

" this will be put call for the same project where i am directly passing
" the doc_id , instid_a and instid_b form read call , will say because the 
" development are inter-dependent if you have to read the gos then you can
" delete the gos using same inputs that you have after reading the gos
" for specific material. 

  DATA : ls_data TYPE zzstructure,
         lt_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zzstructure.
    " Read input from the request
    io_data_provider->read_entry_data( IMPORTING es_data = ls_data ).
    DATA(ls_object_a) = VALUE borident(
        objkey  = ls_data-instid_a
        objtype = ls_data-typeid_a ).

    DATA(ls_object_b) = VALUE borident(
        objkey  = ls_data-instid_b
        objtype = ls_data-typeid_b ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'BINARY_RELATION_DELETE'
      EXPORTING
        obj_rolea          = ls_object_a
        obj_roleb          = ls_object_b
        relationtype       = 'ATTA'
*       FIRE_EVENTS        = 'X'
      EXCEPTIONS
        entry_not_existing = 1
        internal_error     = 2
        no_relation        = 3
        no_role            = 4
        OTHERS             = 5.

    DATA:ls_folder_id TYPE soodk,
         ls_object_id TYPE soodk.

    ls_folder_id-objtp = ls_data-instid_b+0(3).
    ls_folder_id-objyr = ls_data-instid_b+3(2).
    ls_folder_id-objno = ls_data-instid_b+5(12).

    ls_object_id-objtp =  ls_data-instid_b+17(3).
    ls_object_id-objyr =  ls_data-instid_b+20(2).
    ls_object_id-objno =  ls_data-instid_b+22(12).

    CALL FUNCTION 'SO_OBJECT_DELETE'
      EXPORTING
        folder_id                  = ls_folder_id
        object_id                  = ls_object_id
      EXCEPTIONS
        communication_failure      = 1
        folder_not_empty           = 2
        folder_not_exist           = 3
        folder_no_authorization    = 4
        forwarder_not_exist        = 5
        object_not_exist           = 6
        object_no_authorization    = 7
        operation_no_authorization = 8
        owner_not_exist            = 9
        substitute_not_active      = 10
        substitute_not_defined     = 11
        system_failure             = 12
        x_error                    = 13
        OTHERS                     = 14.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      ls_data-message = 'Deleted Successfully !!'.
      ls_data-message_type = 'S'.
      COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
    ELSE.
      ls_data-message = 'Deleted FAILED !!'.
      ls_data-message_type = 'E'.
    ENDIF.

entry.title: Issue Accessing SAP OData v2 API
entry.content:
Hello Community,
I am trying to access the SAP OData v2 API but am not getting a successful response.
OData Service URL I'm trying to access:

https://******.de1.test.crm.cloud.sap/sap/c4c/odata/v2/c4codataapi

I’m using Basic Auth (username/password).
And when using Postman, I get

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" data-wc-hash="e583d7e13177f64bd822698a3bdc78f26f6525e5" data-elastic-env="prod-frankfurt">
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta http-equiv="origin-trial"
      content="AmjP6ZfQVdfo+V//R9rgFI+BwlfMtZ15oYZGOw1FLOPvtr3jTUzLi/KYG7TPp/I6Fp21hidTYDFs77Zt4QMyCgoAAABieyJvcmlnaW4iOiJodHRwczovL2Nsb3VkLnNhcDo0NDMiLCJmZWF0dXJlIjoiVG9wTGV2ZWxUcGNkIiwiZXhwaXJ5IjoxNzM1MzQzOTk5LCJpc1N1YmRvbWFpbiI6dHJ1ZX0=">
    <title></title>
    <base href="/">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
    <link rel="preload" fetchpriority="high" as="image"
        href="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/web-components/e583d7e13177f64bd822698a3bdc78f26f6525e5/assets/images/login/login_image_1.png">
    <link rel="preload" fetchpriority="high" as="image"
        href="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/web-components/e583d7e13177f64bd822698a3bdc78f26f6525e5/assets/images/logo/sap.png">
    <link rel="icon" type="image/x-icon"
        href="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/shell/shell-ui/3ba6f7448ed50bd9227a6b24610af2eca07eab12/favicon.ico">
    <link rel="manifest"
        href="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/shell/shell-ui/3ba6f7448ed50bd9227a6b24610af2eca07eab12/manifest.webmanifest">
    <link rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/web-components/e583d7e13177f64bd822698a3bdc78f26f6525e5/assets/css/css-global.css" />
    <link id="themeLink" rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/web-components/e583d7e13177f64bd822698a3bdc78f26f6525e5/assets/css/css-sap-horizon.css" />
</head>

<body>
    <script type='module'
        src="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/web-components/e583d7e13177f64bd822698a3bdc78f26f6525e5/lib/cxm-components.186092d7.js">
    </script>
    <script
        src="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/shell/shell-lib/d5e71e76eaad2d3969ff8db51d1bb675523192d5/core.v1.js">
    </script>
    <script
        src="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/shell/auth-lib/0bd12b591fbc7279251fb07be9386df99de46bcd/index.umd.js">
    </script>
    <script
        src="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/shell/nav-lib/993531cb314a71b105b66b37dfe8d7b7b9883a53/index.umd.js">
    </script>
    <script
        src="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/coral/atomic-lib/33e3eca102822fcbc288a75a49a3dc47ff94b3d3/index.umd.js">
    </script>
    <crm-shell-ui
        logo="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/web-components/e583d7e13177f64bd822698a3bdc78f26f6525e5/assets/images/logo/sap.png"
        tenant-type="salesAndService"
        img="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/web-components/e583d7e13177f64bd822698a3bdc78f26f6525e5/assets/images/login/login_image_1.png"
        img-failure="false"></crm-shell-ui>
    <script
        src="https://stage.cxm-salescloud.com/mfe/v2/modules/shell/shell-ui/3ba6f7448ed50bd9227a6b24610af2eca07eab12/main.js">
    </script>
    <noscript>Please enable JavaScript to continue using this application.</noscript>
</body>
</html>

Is the service URL correct for accessing the OData v2 API?
Are there any special roles or permissions I need to configure in the SAP tenant?
Is there a way to test or verify that the OData service is exposed and active?

Any help or suggestions would be appreciated!
Thanks in advance.

entry.title: FIORI Url as Oauth2 through OKTA IDP
entry.content:
I am reaching out to seek assistance regarding the configuration of the Fiori URL as OAuth2. I have successfully created a profile through Tcode OA2C_CONFIG and have integrated OKTA as the Identity Provider (IDP). Additionally, I can obtain an Access token via Tcode OA2C_GRANT, and the flow is properly directing to the Fiori URL.

However, I am encountering an issue with the Fiori link that appears following the redirection. When I open the URL in a new private browser session, it initially directs me to the OKTA login details, which is expected. Yet, after redirecting to the Fiori URL, it prompts for SAP credentials. Ideally, the authentication should solely occur via the OKTA token without requiring any SAP credentials.

I would like to bring to your attention that I have already set up the email in the user alias through SU01, which I am using in OKTA.

Additionally, I have experimented with different logon methods for Fiori through SICF. When I selected Basic authentication, it still requests SAP credentials, and for other logon methods, I am receiving an unauthorized error.

Could you please provide guidance or support on resolving this issue? I appreciate your assistance in advance.
